goal: Update website branding with the monkey-like Mainline logo

summary.title: Roll out the monkey-like Mainline logo across the website
summary.what: Added public/mainline_logo.svg, switched the nav and footer brand marks to that asset, added a homepage logo showcase, replaced the favicon SVG and ICO, and regenerated the OG image and social card assets around the new logo.
summary.why: The user provided a new brand mark and asked to use it on the website and in a visible place instead of the temporary M placeholder.
summary.user_goal: Update website branding with the monkey-like Mainline logo

decisions.chose: Use an SVG logo asset as the source of truth.
decisions.rationale: The brand mark is simple and geometric enough to live as a repo-native SVG, which keeps it crisp in navigation, favicon, and social surfaces.

decisions.chose: Show the logo in the navigation, footer, and hero visual instead of only in the browser tab.
decisions.rationale: The user asked for the logo to appear in a suitable place, so the branding now has both persistent chrome and a larger first-viewport showcase.

decisions.chose: Regenerate the social card and OG image around the new logo.
decisions.rationale: Sharing previews should match the updated brand rather than showing the old placeholder mark.

decisions.chose: Expose the logo in Organization and SoftwareApplication JSON-LD.
decisions.rationale: Search and AI systems get a clearer brand entity when the structured data points to the same logo file used on the site.

rejected.reason: That would leave the site visually inconsistent with the new brand asset.
The user asked for the logo to be shown in an appropriate place, not just hidden in tab icons.

review_notes: Built the new logo asset as public/mainline_logo.svg and verified it renders correctly.
Updated public/favicon.svg, public/favicon.ico, public/og-image.png, and public/social-card.svg to use the new logo.
Verified the live homepage HTML contains /mainline_logo.svg in the nav, footer, hero showcase, and JSON-LD logo fields.
Verified https://mainline.sh/mainline_logo.svg, /favicon.svg, /favicon.ico, and /og-image.png all return HTTP 200.
Ran npm run build successfully after the asset and layout changes.
Ran npm run indexnow successfully after deployment.

architectural_claims: public/mainline_logo.svg is the canonical website logo asset.
The website now exposes the same logo across chrome, social previews, and structured data.
behavioral_changes: The top navigation and footer now show the logo image instead of the temporary letter mark.
The homepage hero now includes a larger logo showcase.
The favicon and OG image now match the new logo.
Organization and SoftwareApplication JSON-LD now point to the logo asset.
security_implications: No new user data collection, auth, or tracking behavior was introduced.
migration_notes: If the logo changes again, update public/mainline_logo.svg first, then regenerate the derived favicon and social assets from that source.
